changes.before: By dawn the horses were carrying the night in their flanks...
changes.after: By dawn the horses were dark with dried sweat...

changes.before: Human place. Low beams, smoke in the rafters, one wall furred with nailed-up notices...
changes.after: Low beams. Smoke in the rafters.

changes.before: three months of ordinary commerce hiding inside itself
changes.after: weights, dates, mill marks... sacks spoiled or shorted

changes.before: The clerk's face went blank in the way human faces often did when they understood none of the words but all of the danger.
changes.after: The clerk's face went blank.

changes.before: Ride. Stop. Read. Ask. Ride again.
changes.after: Ride. Stop. Read. Ask.

changes.before: full depot-mistress and merchant vignette
changes.after: single-sentence pass that keeps only the routing clue

changes.before: full yard-master setup with extra stage business
changes.after: tight yard-master setup that keeps competence pressure

changes.before: some settlements had men gone from work and bakeries complaining...
changes.after: others had men gone from work and too many sick customers at once

changes.before: The understanding landed ugly and clean.
changes.after: cut; the siege-work realization now stands on its own

changes.before: the place had the shape of a working depot and the silence of an emptied chapel
changes.after: the place had the shape of a working depot and nothing alive enough to prove it

changes.before: The first read was almost tidy. Too tidy.
changes.after: chair, cup, and drawer details carry the suspicion without the gloss

changes.before: If they were sloppy. / They were hurried. That's better.
changes.after: kept only the exchange that earns the clue

changes.before: formal, expensive, made for people who did not touch their own freight if they could pay others to do it
changes.after: formal, expensive, made for people who paid others to touch freight

changes.before: Barov clue could have tipped into accusation jargon
changes.after: kept Jaina on ownership / lease / factors and Sylvanas on proof first

changes.before: the sense afterward that she had been offered something ordinary and filed it where ordinary things went
changes.after: the sense afterward that she had been offered something ordinary and forgotten it

changes.before: Then we file him harder and keep moving.
changes.after: Keep him in mind and move on.

changes.before: Not the mage had marked this poorly.
changes.after: Not the mage... Jaina had marked this poorly.

changes.before: expanded explanation of her script, speech, and coddling logic
changes.after: shorter name-shift paragraph that keeps the trust movement

changes.before: That isn't sleep. That's cavalry lying to itself.
changes.after: kept; plainspoken and character-true

changes.before: full morning staging paragraph with extra explanation
changes.after: compressed departure beat with orders, sacks, and road choice only

observations.note: Sylvanas POV stayed threat-first; rewrites preserved clipped tactical framing instead of adding interior gloss.
Jaina's early route logic already fit her voice sheet: built thought, plain language, no modern lab jargon.
The chapter function was clear: road-trace -> depot knot -> cleaned trail -> south decision. Cuts were limited to prose slack, not beat loss.
The chapter's emotional payload is the quiet shift from 'the mage' to 'Jaina'; that beat had to survive every compression pass.
The ending needed to stay forward-driving and procedural; extra departure description was safe to kill.
Canon check: Andorhal as Lordaeron grain hub is accurate and worth preserving as the investigation's center.
Canon check: Strahnbrad and Arthas's current theatre align with the Warcraft III opening campaign.
Canon check: Barov ownership in western Lordaeron is plausible as reach / holdings, but not automatic guilt; the dialogue now keeps that distinction explicit.
The odd farewell 'May the stars keep your road' reads as story-canon clue, not Blizzard canon error; kept intact as a planted tell.
No geography or role contradiction surfaced in the road-to-Andorhal chain, so research drove restraint rather than lore correction.
Word count dropped from 5411 to 4327, beating the 20% cut target without removing any chapter event.
Inn setup lost wall-notice clutter and kept only the ledger confrontation that matters.
The west-fork / midday montage repeated the same stop-read-ask loop; I compressed duplicate logistics beats and kept only route-turning information.
The Andorhal knot scene kept its full clue payload but lost ornamental filler around the yard master and weaker complaint details.
The stripped depot sequence was dense enough to deserve a reread; cuts targeted only repeated suspicion signals and over-signposting.
The coaching-house planning pass stayed long by design because it carries the chapter's trust shift and route conclusion.
Departure staging was over-written relative to function; it now lands on orders, intent, and motion.
Killed narrator hand-holding on the clerk's fear; the blank face is enough.
Killed abstract-noun locomotion by removing the sentence that labeled understanding instead of letting the siege inference do the work.
Replaced chapel-register comparison with a cleaner tactical read of dead silence and stripped activity.
The corpse scene was flirting with tidy-case over-explanation; I left the physical tells and cut the editorial wink.
The brazier clue sequence now stays on ash, wax, and route fragments instead of repeating that the enemy moved fast.
The post-Barov exchange kept subtext and threat but lost explanation that the reader already had.
Merchant-memory and name-shift paragraphs had database-language and meta-commentary; both were cut back to concrete recall and recognition.
Removed expression-scaffolding around Sylvanas almost smiling; the banter now lands flatter and cleaner.
Hitlist counts improved materially: like 6->3, as if 2->1, just 7->3, not 79->53, felt 2->0.
Sylvanas's spoken lines stayed command-short, never bureaucratic or report-like.
Jaina's explanatory lines stayed grounded in flour, routes, households, and dependence; no vector / diagnostic / actionable jargon slipped in.
Necromancy diagnosis stayed plain enough for speech: residue, deliberate, buried, ordinary. No lab-report diction.
Barov discussion kept legal-commercial vocabulary that fit the moment and the speakers, without slipping into institutional memo voice.
The sleep banter stayed plain-language character voice; no modern smart-talk contamination.

researchFindings: Andorhal's grain-hub role is canon-accurate and supports the chapter's route logic.
Strahnbrad as Arthas's current theatre is canon-consistent for this story moment.
Barov holdings plausibly connect to western Lordaeron freight, but the seal is evidence of reach, not proof of culpability.
flags: Line 319: Barov kept as a partial seal clue only; the text explicitly refuses to overclaim guilt.
Line 489: 'May the stars keep your road' kept because it is a planted recurring tell, not slop.
Dialogue hunt found zero document-register violations in quoted speech after cleanup.
